steps.title: Spot the parallel requirement
steps.content: ”Enacted in 2010 and ______ three of these features” joins two modifiers of constitution with “and.” The first modifier uses the past participle enacted; the blank must provide a participle to match this structure.

steps.title: Apply the parallelism rule
steps.content:
Containing is a participle that matches enacted structurally, allowing both phrases to modify constitution as equal adjectives. The other three—had contained, contained, contains—are full verb forms with tense, which disrupt the parallel structure.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
